programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Instructor Recert Course
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This in-person recertification course focuses on the competencies learned in the instructor, coach and examiner courses and provides a refresher to those competencies. Course content will be updated every two years. This course provides the 4 points required to complete the leadership recertification for instructors, coaches, examiners and examiner mentors. This course does not automatically recertify a candidate. Following successful course completion candidates must complete the recertification process to be recertified.Prerequisite: Current Swim Instructor and/or Lifesaving Instructor and/or Examiners and/or any Coach Certification. Candidates must bring their instructor course materials to this recert course. These may include: Swim For Life Award Guide or Toronto Learn to Swim Manual, Instructor Manual, Swim Patrol Award Guide, Bronze Medals Award Guide, First Aid Award Guide, Teaching Swim for Life.Note: This course can be completed at any time during your 24 month currency period and leadership recertification online form submitted with payment when your awards are expiring.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Introduction to Machine Knitting: Bags and Home Decor
programs.description: Using our LK150 knitting machines and your own yarn, create your custom designs like bags, pillowcases and more! Students will learnthe fundamentals of machine knitting including skills like casting on and off, forming slip knots and stitches, and checking tension. Thisworkshop requires an additional $15 material fee (cash only), payable to instructor for practice materials. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Introduction to Oil Painting
programs.description: Learn to work with oil paints, with this step-by step introduction to guiding you through a range of foundational techniques and Approaches. Participants will learn how to plan and complete an oil painting while exploring key concepts such as underpainting, fat over-thin, glazing, impasto and alla prima. Designed for beginners, the course provides a solid foundation in both the materials and methods of oil painting in a supportive, hands-on environment. Please note that only non-toxic, solvent-free mediums and thinners are permitted.

programs.name: Introduction to Punch Needle Rug Hooking
programs.description: Discover the art of punch  needle rug hooking in this fun, hands-on one-day workshop! Learn the basics of  the technique, including how to use the punch needle tool, set up your fabric,  and create textured loops. You’ll design and complete a small, personalized piece.  This workshop includes a $30 material fee payable to the instructor.

programs.name: Introduction to Weaving
programs.description: Whether you're a complete beginner or looking  to refine your skills, this course is designed to guide you through the  fundamentals and creative possibilities of weaving. You'll learn the essentials  of weaving techniques, including loom setup, winding the warp, basic weave  structures, finishing techniques, terminology, loom descriptions and how to  read weaving patterns. Participants are welcome to bring their own yarns and  projects ideas. For  those who are new to weaving, beginner projects will be provided and yarn can  be purchased from the instructor, for a fee between $20-30.

programs.name: Japanese Paper Marbling
programs.description: Create beautiful, marbled patterns on different  types of paper in this exciting workshop. The patterns are the result of color  floated on a liquid, and then carefully transferred to paper. Students will be  able to take home and use their unique creations. A materials fee in cash of  $10.00 will be due to the course instructor at the start of the class.

programs.name: Instructor Recert Course
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This in-person recertification course focuses on the competencies learned in the instructor, coach and examiner courses and provides a refresher to those competencies. Course content will be updated every two years. This course provides the 4 points required to complete the leadership recertification for instructors, coaches, examiners and examiner mentors. This course does not automatically recertify a candidate. Following successful course completion candidates must complete the recertification process to be recertified.Prerequisite: Current Swim Instructor and/or Lifesaving Instructor and/or Examiners and/or any Coach Certification. Candidates must bring their instructor course materials to this recert course. These may include: Swim For Life Award Guide or Toronto Learn to Swim Manual, Instructor Manual, Swim Patrol Award Guide, Bronze Medals Award Guide, First Aid Award Guide, Teaching Swim for Life.Note: This course can be completed at any time during your 24 month currency period and leadership recertification online form submitted with payment when your awards are expiring.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Introduction to Machine Knitting: Bags and Home Decor
programs.description: Using our LK150 knitting machines and your own yarn, create your custom designs like bags, pillowcases and more! Students will learnthe fundamentals of machine knitting including skills like casting on and off, forming slip knots and stitches, and checking tension. Thisworkshop requires an additional $15 material fee (cash only), payable to instructor for practice materials. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Introduction to Oil Painting
programs.description: Learn to work with oil paints, with this step-by step introduction to guiding you through a range of foundational techniques and Approaches. Participants will learn how to plan and complete an oil painting while exploring key concepts such as underpainting, fat over-thin, glazing, impasto and alla prima. Designed for beginners, the course provides a solid foundation in both the materials and methods of oil painting in a supportive, hands-on environment. Please note that only non-toxic, solvent-free mediums and thinners are permitted.

programs.name: Introduction to Punch Needle Rug Hooking
programs.description: Discover the art of punch  needle rug hooking in this fun, hands-on one-day workshop! Learn the basics of  the technique, including how to use the punch needle tool, set up your fabric,  and create textured loops. You’ll design and complete a small, personalized piece.  This workshop includes a $30 material fee payable to the instructor.

programs.name: Introduction to Weaving
programs.description: Whether you're a complete beginner or looking  to refine your skills, this course is designed to guide you through the  fundamentals and creative possibilities of weaving. You'll learn the essentials  of weaving techniques, including loom setup, winding the warp, basic weave  structures, finishing techniques, terminology, loom descriptions and how to  read weaving patterns. Participants are welcome to bring their own yarns and  projects ideas. For  those who are new to weaving, beginner projects will be provided and yarn can  be purchased from the instructor, for a fee between $20-30.

programs.name: Japanese Paper Marbling
programs.description: Create beautiful, marbled patterns on different  types of paper in this exciting workshop. The patterns are the result of color  floated on a liquid, and then carefully transferred to paper. Students will be  able to take home and use their unique creations. A materials fee in cash of  $10.00 will be due to the course instructor at the start of the class.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2 - Small Group
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. Class size is limited to four participants. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

Water play or swim days will also be enjoyed, dependent on weather and camp location. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.
